The 7-Digit Doubt Destroyer (Completed 05/08/18)

Page 1 of 2
Speaker 1: All right guys. Welcome back. This is going to be Themeplate 16 called the 7-Digit Doubt
Destroyer, and this is where seven digits are worth a thousand words because by giving
your phone number on one of your final closes, it gives the sense that you're a trusted
friend. Friends give friends their phone number. You're a real normal human being. You
have an iPhone, you have an Android, whatever you're using, and you have a number.
You're safe. You won't run away with their money, and a decision is needed. That's how
we're going to be framing this. You wouldn't leave a friend hanging. If someone said,
"I'm waiting for you. Here's my number. Let me know where you're at," kind of deal,
you'd feel very compelled to answer that because we don't leave friends hanging.
The other thing we're doing here on a more subtle level is creating this condition that
not enrolling requires a phone call, which actually adds a little bit of friction to non-
buying compared and contrasted to the ease around enrolling. So, that is what we are
working to activate in The 7-Digit Doubt Destroyer. That's how it's playing its role in
coaching decisiveness in that final day. So I would recommend sending this as usually
one of the final closes the evening of your cart closing. So, you will open with a reminder
of the urgency, just letting them know where they're at as far as the closing of your
launch or promotion, future paste the immediate ownership or experience in buying. So
this is done to make it less scary, to make purchase feel more safe.
Normalize or even glorify procrastination. These people at this point are the
procrastinators. By procrasti-shaming as most marketers would do here, you're actually
alienating the only people left in the room. So, instead poke fun at it, normalize it, even
glorify it to a certain degree. Then you can add your call to action, have your sources of
exclusive empowerment, minimum viable commitment, and then you can offer a phone
call, if they're on the fence. Drop your digits, and close with that quickest, most valuable
win. So, this is what it would look like. Why not dial me up? Hey, looks like we got just
about an hour left before we close up shop on 10X launches. So, urgency right off the
bat without painting it on too thick with the [inaudible 00:02:33]. We already have
about X new members running around the membership site, putting the themeplates to
profitable use, and exchanging virtual fist bumps inside the Facebook group. It's
awesome.
Anyway, thought I'd pull myself away from that party and see what's on your mind. If
you're the adventurous type and just wanted to wait til the final minutes before ninja
rolling into the membership area, then and we can add a gift of that membership area,
and those real conversations happening there. Here's your last chance to do the best
Indian Jones impression.
So, we're not procrasti-shaming. Instead, we're kind of showing, it's kind of cool here.
It's kind of adventurous for wanting to do it that way. But if you have an online product
you're proud of, have an email list of over a 150 subscribers, we'd be happy to hear
from you. Have about 60 minutes this week to fire off your first profitable themeplates.
So these are all sources of all circumstantial exclusive empowerment, and still don't feel
like you'd be in a position to put 10X launches to a 60 day risk-free test to see if it's
worth it. So, here's your minimum viable commitment, then I'd super appreciate it if
you'd dial me up to let me know what's on your mind. Not a sales call by any stretch,

Figure I owe you that much for spending all this time with me over the last few weeks,
and this is totally sincere. On one level, it's reminding the prospect of the journey that

consistency principle, and tightening that consistency loop, but at the same time, I'm
serious. I do kind of owe you that much for all this time you spent over the last few

most products owners and online entrepreneurs are able to at least double their
investment within a few hours. As long as you have a validated offer and an email list,
however small, to send it to you, all you really need is about 50 minutes of poking
around module 7 to pick out your favorite sales themeplate and fire it into your email
provider. So, we're closing with that quickest most valuable win.
Being very specific. 50 minutes. Poking around module 7, which I know in a very meta
kind of way we're in right now. We're literally poking around it. If you're up for the test
and you'd rather chat with me on Facebook then on the phone, go ahead and take the
50 minute ... On the Facebook group, sorry. Then on the phone, go ahead and take the
50 minute ROI challenge above, then reach out to let me know your in. So that's it.
We're making buying a little more frictionless, since that would involve just kind of a
Facebook chat messages, which most people would be more comfortable with than
getting on the phone.
So, we're kind of taking some of the stress in the buying decision, and putting that stress
in not buying. So not buying is more stressful than buying. It's just transferring where
that friction is. That is The 7-Digit Doubt Destroyer, and it's a powerful email. Putting
your phone number there. A lot of people will have objections to doing. I'm not saying
you have to do it. This isn't one of those essential, you need to plug it in right now,
themeplates, but I would encourage you to do it at least once because even putting
your number out there, and hearing about people who aren't ready to buy and coaching
them, literally having a chance to try to coach them into the conversion on the spot or
at least listen. I'm not even saying it's a sales call, but at least listen to what's holding
them back can be more insightful and profitable in the long-term than a sales call. So, do
it. If it speaks to you, put your number. Don't be afraid of it. Communicate with your
prospects. Communicate with people you've yet to ethically persuade over the buying
line because that's where you're going to unlock so many more additional insights.
So that is it for The 7-Digit Doubt Destroyer. Sorry, we got a few more templates left to
go. So if you're going to be working on this one, take about 20 minutes right now, jot it
down, and meet me back for themeplate 17 in just a few moments. See you there.
